$schema: Each entry is a primary-source-cited fact. If you cannot link a primary source, do not add it.

world_population.note: Used as the divisor for every per-capita calculation on this site.

world_gdp_nominal_usd.note: Nominal, current prices. PPP totals run higher (~$185T).

world_household_wealth_usd.note: 47.5% of this wealth is held by the 58 million people worth >$1M.

global_military_spending_usd.source_name: SIPRI, Trends in World Military Expenditure 2024
global_military_spending_usd.note: 10th consecutive annual increase; 2.5% of world GDP.

global_health_spending_usd.note: 9.9% of GDP. High-income countries account for 79%; the US alone is 43%.

world_cereal_production_tonnes.source_name: FAO Cereal Supply and Demand Brief
world_cereal_production_tonnes.note: Cereals alone — does not include legumes, tubers, vegetables, fruit, meat, dairy, fish, oil crops.

daily_food_supply_kcal_per_capita.source_name: FAOSTAT Food Balance Sheets / Our World in Data
daily_food_supply_kcal_per_capita.note: Total dietary energy available for human consumption. Excludes feed, seed, industrial use, and post-harvest loss.

people_in_hunger.source_name: FAO State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World 2025
people_in_hunger.note: Point estimate (range 638–720M). Hunger is rising in Africa and Western Asia even as it declined globally.

food_loss_and_waste_share.source_name: FAO + UNEP Food Waste Index Report 2024
food_loss_and_waste_share.note: ~13% lost post-harvest before retail; ~19% wasted at retail/food-service/household.

global_electricity_generation_twh.note: IEA reports 2024 generation grew ~1,200 TWh (4%) over 2023's 29,471 TWh. Renewables + nuclear covered 40% in 2024.

renewable_freshwater_km3.note: Total long-term renewable internal freshwater resources globally. Distribution is geographically uneven.

homeless_people.source_name: UN-Habitat / UN DESA, World Social Summit 2025 background
homeless_people.note: UN-Habitat acknowledges global homelessness data is incomplete and likely understated.

inadequate_housing_people.note: Lacks access to adequate housing, secure land tenure, or basic water/sanitation services. ~40% of the global population.

extreme_poverty_count.source_name: World Bank, March 2026 Global Poverty Update
extreme_poverty_count.note: Threshold raised from $2.15 (2017 PPP) to $3.00/day (2021 PPP) in June 2025.

extreme_poverty_line_usd_per_day.source_name: World Bank, June 2025 Update to Global Poverty Lines
extreme_poverty_line_usd_per_day.note: Replaces the prior $2.15/day (2017 PPP) International Poverty Line.

minimum_calorie_need_kcal.source_name: FAO/WHO/UNU Joint Expert Consultation, Human Energy Requirements
minimum_calorie_need_kcal.note: Standard reference for an average adult; varies by age, sex, body size, activity.

minimum_water_need_l_per_day.source_name: WHO, Domestic Water Quantity, Service Level and Health
minimum_water_need_l_per_day.note: Intermediate access level: drinking, cooking, hygiene. Survival minimum is 7.5 L/day; full health protection ~100 L/day.

tier1_electricity_kwh_per_year.note: Urban household minimum (500 kWh/household ÷ 5). Lights, phone charging, radio, fan.

modern_energy_minimum_kwh_per_year.source_name: Rockefeller Foundation / Energy for Growth Hub — Modern Energy Minimum
modern_energy_minimum_kwh_per_year.note: Proposed threshold including productive/commercial use, not just residential.

insulin_generic_cost_per_pen_usd.unit: USD per prefilled human insulin pen
insulin_generic_cost_per_pen_usd.source_name: Barber, Sulis et al. (2024). 'Estimated Sustainable Cost-Based Prices for Diabetes Medicines.' JAMA Network Open / MSF Access Campaign
insulin_generic_cost_per_pen_usd.note: Cost-based price (production cost + small margin) for a generic human-insulin pen. US list price for the same is ~$90.

insulin_us_pen_price_usd.unit: USD per prefilled human insulin pen (US list price)
insulin_us_pen_price_usd.source_name: Barber, Sulis et al. (2024), JAMA Network Open / MSF
insulin_us_pen_price_usd.note: Same molecule as the $0.94 cost-based price. Markup ~96x.

global_adults_with_diabetes.source_name: International Diabetes Federation, IDF Diabetes Atlas 11th edition (2024)
global_adults_with_diabetes.note: 1 in 9 adults. 43% (252M) are undiagnosed. 81% live in low- and middle-income countries.

global_internet_users.note: 68% of world population. 2.6B remain offline; 1.8B of those live in rural areas.

international_internet_bandwidth_tbps.note: Inter-region installed capacity. CAGR 24% since 2020. Doesn't include intra-region fiber.

global_internet_traffic_zb_per_year.note: Fixed broadband ~6 ZB + mobile ~1.3 ZB. Per-capita averaged = ~890 GB/person/year.

global_dtp3_immunization_coverage.unit: fraction of one-year-old infants completing 3-dose DTP series
global_dtp3_immunization_coverage.source_name: WHO/UNICEF Estimates of National Immunization Coverage (WUENIC) 2024 revision
global_dtp3_immunization_coverage.note: 89% got at least one dose. WHO target is 90%. ~109M children fully immunized; ~14M zero-dose.

global_unvaccinated_children.unit: infants worldwide with zero doses of DTP
global_unvaccinated_children.note: The 'zero-dose' population — never received any routine immunization. Concentrated in conflict and fragile-state contexts.

safely_managed_sanitation_share.unit: fraction of world population using safely managed sanitation services
safely_managed_sanitation_share.source_name: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme (JMP), Progress on household drinking water, sanitation and hygiene 2000-2022
safely_managed_sanitation_share.note: 57% (about 4.5 billion people) had safely managed sanitation in 2022; 3.5 billion lacked it, of whom 419 million still practise open defecation. 'Safely managed' is the highest service rung (excreta safely treated/disposed), so the share with any basic toilet is higher.

safely_managed_drinking_water_share.unit: fraction of world population using safely managed drinking-water services
safely_managed_drinking_water_share.source_name: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme (JMP), Progress on household drinking water, sanitation and hygiene 2000-2022
safely_managed_drinking_water_share.note: 73% had safely managed drinking water in 2022; 2.2 billion lacked it, including 115 million who still drink untreated surface water. This is a service-access measure and is distinct from the renewable-freshwater resource volume entry, which shows the planet has ~5,000+ m3 of renewable freshwater per person per year.

clean_cooking_polluting_fuel_people.unit: people cooking with polluting fuels and open fires
clean_cooking_polluting_fuel_people.source_name: WHO, Household air pollution fact sheet (updated 16 December 2025)
clean_cooking_polluting_fuel_people.note: Around 2.1 billion people (about a quarter of humanity) still cook with kerosene, biomass (wood, dung, crop waste) or coal on open fires or inefficient stoves. The resulting household air pollution caused an estimated 2.9 million deaths in 2021, including over 309,000 children under 5. Clean stoves and LPG/electric cooking are mature, manufactured at scale.

out_of_school_children.unit: children and youth out of school worldwide
out_of_school_children.source_name: UNESCO, Global Education Monitoring Report 2024 (UIS estimates)
out_of_school_children.note: 251 million children and youth were out of school as of UNESCO's October 2024 report. The out-of-school population has fallen only about 1% in nearly a decade; 33% of school-age children in low-income countries are out of school versus 3% in high-income countries. A later 2025 UIS dashboard revision raised the count to 272 million as more data arrived.

maternal_deaths_preventable.unit: maternal deaths worldwide in 2023, nearly all preventable
maternal_deaths_preventable.source_name: WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA, World Bank Group and UNDESA/Population Division, Trends in Maternal Mortality 2000 to 2023 (2025)
maternal_deaths_preventable.note: An estimated 260,000 women died of maternal causes in 2023, about 712 per day or one roughly every two minutes, from largely preventable causes related to pregnancy and childbirth. Maternal mortality fell about 40% from 2000 to 2023. Sub-Saharan Africa and Southern Asia accounted for about 87% (225,000) of the deaths.

essential_medicines_public_availability.unit: lower bound of mean generic-medicine availability in the public sector across WHO regions (range 37.8%-68.3%)
essential_medicines_public_availability.source_name: Oldfield et al., 'Prices, availability, and affordability of adult medicines in 54 low-income and middle-income countries', Lancet Global Health (2025)
essential_medicines_public_availability.note: Across 54 low- and middle-income countries, mean availability of generic medicines in the public sector ranged from 37.8% to 68.3% by WHO region (private sector 42.3%-77.4%). No region's public OR private sector met WHO's 80% availability target. Patients paid 3.0-11.5 times international reference prices for the cheapest generics.
